title: cerastium aquaticum var. scandens
intro: Cerastium aquaticum var. scandens, also known as the creeping or scandent water chickweed, is a type of flowering plant that is native to North America and Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Cerastium aquaticum var. scandens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in herbal remedies for centuries.
